tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is mildly impaired (slightly elevated serum creatinine), with hyperchloremia. Urinalysis shows proteinuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Low HbA1c suggests possible anemia or altered red cell turnover. Findings may indicate early renal dysfunction and possible dehydration or catabolic state; clinical correlation and further evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
